companies.notes: Annual corporate dinner client. Second year with us.

companies.notes: Fundraiser gala Apr 18. Off-site catering at museum.

companies.name: St. Boniface Church
companies.notes: Lenten fish fry partnership. Long-standing community relationship.

companies.name: Nakamura Medical Group
companies.notes: Dr. Nakamura birthday dinner Apr 5. Referred by Froedtert colleague.

companies.name: UWM Alumni Association
companies.notes: Alumni reception May 15. First event with us — growth opportunity.

companies.name: Baird Financial Group
companies.notes: Corporate retreat dinner Jun 7. High-profile client, VP-level contacts.

companies.name: Hartwell & Associates
companies.notes: VIP corporate regulars. Monthly partner dinners, table for 6. Prefers back corner booth.

companies.name: Lakefront Ventures
companies.notes: VIP regulars. Weekly Friday dinner for 4. Wine club members. Spend avg $600/visit.

contacts.name: Lisa Chen
contacts.title: Bride
contacts.notes: Very responsive via email. Fiancé: David. Mother-in-law has shellfish allergy.

contacts.name: Lisa Johnson
contacts.title: VP of Operations
contacts.notes: Decision-maker for all corporate events. Prefers email communication. Gluten-free.

contacts.name: Sarah Lindgren
contacts.title: Director of Special Events
contacts.notes: Coordinating museum access and setup logistics. Very detail-oriented.

contacts.name: Patricia Alvarez
contacts.notes: Mother of quinceañera. Bilingual (Spanish/English). Coordinating cake and mariachi separately.

contacts.name: Father Thomas Nowak
contacts.title: Parish Priest
contacts.notes: Great community partner. Congregation feedback has been very positive on fish fry. May want to extend series next year.

contacts.name: Dr. Reiko Nakamura
contacts.title: Chief of Cardiology, Froedtert Hospital
contacts.notes: Foodie, interested in Japanese-Italian fusion concept. Turning 60. Several guests are sake enthusiasts.

contacts.name: Karen Whitfield
contacts.title: Events Coordinator
contacts.notes: Working within institutional budget. Responsive to email.

contacts.name: David Bernstein
contacts.title: VP of Events & Client Relations
contacts.notes: Premium expectations. Interested in sommelier-led wine education segment. Budget is flexible.

contacts.name: Patricia Hartwell
contacts.title: Managing Partner
contacts.notes: VIP regular. Monthly partner dinner, usually first Tuesday. Prefers back corner booth. Sends wine as holiday gift.

contacts.name: James Okafor
contacts.title: Founding Partner
contacts.notes: VIP regular. Weekly Friday dinner. Wine club member — enjoys Barolo tastings with Maria. Potential catering lead for firm's summer retreat.

contacts.name: David
contacts.title: Groom
contacts.notes: Lisa's fiancé. Handling AV and music logistics for reception. More relaxed than Lisa about details.

contacts.name: Sofia Alvarez
contacts.notes: Quinceañera honoree. Patricia's daughter. 15th birthday celebration.

deals.name: Chen Wedding Reception — Jun 14
deals.notes: Total estimated $10,400. 120 guests. Deposit $5,200 received. Second installment due May 1.

deals.name: Johnson Corp Annual Dinner — May 2
deals.notes: Full buyout. Estimated $17K. 50% deposit received. Final balance due 2 weeks before event.

deals.name: Milwaukee Art Museum Gala — Apr 18
deals.notes: Off-site at museum. 150 guests. Deposit received. Final invoice sent 3/15 — awaiting payment.

deals.name: Alvarez Quinceañera — Mar 22
deals.notes: 100 guests at Bella Notte (private dining + outdoor terrace). Event complete. Deposit + final balance paid. Patricia expressed interest in future events.

deals.name: St. Boniface Lenten Fish Fry — Feb–Apr
deals.notes: Ongoing series. 5 of 7 Fridays complete. Deposit covers full series. Final reconciliation after last Friday.

deals.name: Dr. Nakamura Birthday Dinner — Apr 5
deals.notes: Japanese-Italian fusion tasting menu. 7 courses. Tony developing concept. 40 guests.

deals.name: UWM Alumni Reception — May 15
deals.notes: Cocktail reception, heavy apps + open bar. 160 guests. Menu draft sent — awaiting feedback.

deals.name: Baird Financial Retreat Dinner — Jun 7
deals.notes: Premium event. 90 guests. Dietary survey sent. Wine education segment under discussion with Maria.

engagements.body: Inbound inquiry from Lisa Johnson at Johnson Corp. She attended a holiday party here last year and wants to book the annual corporate dinner. 120 guests, full restaurant buyout. Discussed May 2 date. Sending proposal this week.

Sent catering proposal to Lisa Johnson for Johnson Corp annual dinner. Included 4-course Northern Italian prix fixe options and cocktail hour packages. She wants to review with CEO before confirming menu.

Patricia Hartwell's October partner dinner. Table for 6, back corner booth, 7:30 PM. They ordered the Barolo and the veal osso buco as always. Patricia mentioned they may want to host a holiday client reception in December. Following up.

Exploratory call from David Bernstein at Baird Financial. Interested in hosting a corporate retreat dinner for 90 people in June. Wants something upscale but not stuffy. Discussed private dining + patio option. Sending venue info.

Initial tasting meeting with Lisa Chen and David. Discussed family-style Italian for 120 guests. Lisa's mother-in-law has shellfish allergy — noted for menu. Toured restaurant and patio. Very excited about the space. Deposit agreement signed.

Sent formal proposal to David Bernstein for Baird Financial retreat dinner. Premium prix fixe concept with sommelier-led wine education segment. He's very interested. Budget is flexible — wants memorable.

Call from Karen Whitfield at UWM Alumni Association. Looking for a cocktail reception venue for 160 graduates. May 15 date. Discussed heavy apps + open bar format. She's working within institutional budget. Sending initial pricing.
Sent Chen wedding deposit invoice ($5,200). Lisa confirmed receipt. Payment expected by end of month.
James Okafor's Friday dinner. He mentioned Lakefront Ventures is growing and they may need a venue for investor events. Noted for follow-up in Q1. Weekly spend continues strong at ~$600.

Sat down with Father Thomas Nowak at St. Boniface to discuss Lenten fish fry partnership. He wants beer-battered cod, coleslaw, fries — classic Wisconsin fish fry. Pickup format from our kitchen, church volunteers serve at parish hall. 7 consecutive Fridays, Feb 20 through Apr 3. Agreed on $2,400 deposit covering the series.
Sent St. Boniface fish fry deposit invoice ($2,400). Father Nowak says the parish council approved the budget. Check coming by Dec 15.
Lisa Chen called to confirm deposit payment was sent. Guest count holding at 120. She asked about late-night pizza station — Tony thinks we can do it. Will mock up add-on pricing.

Site visit at Milwaukee Art Museum with Sarah Lindgren. Walked the atrium and Windhover Hall spaces. Confirmed power access for chafing stations and bar setup. Loading dock available from 2 PM for setup. Sarah sending floor plan by end of week.
Sent Milwaukee Art Museum deposit invoice ($6,800). Sarah confirmed receipt. Payment processing through museum accounting — expect 7-10 business days.

Patricia Alvarez called about quinceañera for her daughter Sofia. 100 guests. Wants it at Bella Notte — private dining room plus outdoor terrace. March 22 date. Discussed menu: antipasto, chicken marsala, pasta primavera, tiramisu. She's handling mariachi and tres leches cake separately. Father Nowak referred her.
Sent Alvarez quinceañera proposal and deposit invoice ($3,200). 100 guests, private dining + outdoor terrace at Bella Notte. Patricia confirmed she'll pay by mid-January.

First meeting with Dr. Reiko Nakamura about her 60th birthday dinner. April 5. She wants something special — Japanese-Italian fusion concept. 40 guests. Main dining room buyout. She brought a sake she'd like incorporated. Tony developing a 7-course menu concept.
Sent Dr. Nakamura deposit invoice ($4,100). She paid same week via credit card. Very excited about the fusion concept.
Sent formal catering proposal to Johnson Corp. Updated pricing with cocktail hour option. Lisa Johnson reviewing with CEO.
Sent UWM Alumni reception proposal and deposit invoice ($7,200) to Karen Whitfield. Cocktail-style, 200 guests, heavy apps + open bar.
Sent Baird Financial deposit invoice ($7,600). David Bernstein confirmed — paid via credit card within the week.
Fish fry series underway. Week 1 (Feb 20): 65 portions. Father Nowak called after — congregation loved it. Best fish fry in years, he said. Smooth operation.

Valentine's Day — packed house, 262 covers. Patricia Hartwell's group came in as a couples dinner instead of the usual partner dinner. Ordered three bottles of Barolo. Big night for the bar — Maria ran a special cocktail menu. Record revenue night.

Father Nowak called to discuss fish fry adjustments. Congregation wants more fries. Increasing portion by 10% for next week. Also mentioned possibility of extending partnership beyond Lent for monthly parish dinners.

Spoke with David Bernstein about Baird retreat dinner logistics. He wants a 'sophisticated but approachable' evening. Interested in sommelier-led wine education — Maria could walk guests through a 4-wine Italian tasting during appetizer course. Dietary survey going out to 90 attendees this week.

Menu development meeting with Dr. Nakamura at restaurant. Tony presented 7-course concept: amuse-bouche (dashi-infused burrata), miso black cod, yuzu risotto, wagyu tagliata, etc. She loved it. Deciding between matcha tiramisu or traditional cassata for dessert. Robert at Capital Wine to suggest Barolo pairings.

Sent initial menu draft to Karen Whitfield for UWM Alumni reception. Proposed: passed bruschetta trio, arancini, antipasto stations, pasta station, carving station (porchetta), and dessert display. Open bar with Prosecco, local craft beer (Lakefront Brewery), and signature Negroni.
James Okafor's Friday dinner — mentioned at the table that Lakefront Ventures is planning a summer retreat for ~50 people. Asked if we do off-site. Told him absolutely. Follow up next week.

Patricia Alvarez called to finalize quinceañera details. Confirmed 100 guests. External bakery delivering tres leches cake by 5 PM day-of. Mariachi band (Mariachi Sol de Milwaukee) arriving at 7 PM. Final headcount locked. Outdoor terrace confirmed weather permitting.
Sent final invoice to Milwaukee Art Museum (INV-3009). Final balance of $6,800 due by April 10. Sarah confirmed receipt and said accounting will process within 10 business days.

Follow-up email to James Okafor RE: Lakefront Ventures summer retreat catering. Attached catering brochure and sample menus. Offered complimentary tasting for his team. He replied same day — interested, will discuss with partners.

Quick call from Lisa Johnson. She wants to add a cocktail hour with passed hors d'oeuvres before the sit-down dinner. Asked for pricing on 45 min open bar + 4 app selections for 120. Sending updated proposal by end of week.

Lisa Chen called with guest update — holding at 120. Her cousin is vegan, plus 3 vegetarian guests. Need a clear vegan option for the family-style spread. Also wants the late-night pizza station confirmed. Tony can make it work. Second deposit installment coming May 1.

Sent updated Johnson Corp proposal with cocktail hour add-on. 45-minute open bar (premium rail) + passed bruschetta, arancini, prosciutto-wrapped figs, and burrata crostini. Additional $3,200 to the quoted total. Lisa reviewing with her team.

Post-event debrief — Alvarez quinceañera. Event went beautifully. Patricia was emotional, very happy. Mariachi band was a hit. Tres leches cake arrived on time. Terrace worked well despite evening chill — we set up heat lamps. Only issue: bar ran low on horchata. Patricia mentioned her sister-in-law is planning a baptism party for fall — potential lead.

Karen Whitfield emailed back about UWM menu. Loves the concept but asked if we can substitute porchetta with a lighter option — many alumni are older demographic. Suggested roasted branzino station instead. Also wants a non-alcoholic cocktail option. Will revise and resend by 4/1.

Dr. Nakamura texted to confirm matcha tiramisu as the dessert course. Full 7-course menu is now locked. Need to place specialty orders with Sysco (wagyu) and Capital Wine (sake) by 3/30 at latest. Tony handling kitchen prep timeline.
